text:
Starscream is a Decepticon from the ' series. His spirit possesses an Angel during a attack on Tokyo-3 and becomes the monstrous Angel-Scream. 

Profile

The Angel-Scream is the result of the possession of an Angel by the ghost of the deceased Decepticon Starscream. His appearance remains similar to the one he had before, but he has a number of unique physical characteristics. His fingers lengthened and his shoulders took on the appearance of that of the Fourth Angel. His cabin is entirely black and an Angel mask is visible on his bust. In this form, Starscream retains its personality. He is eager for power and keeps his memories, especially about his death at the hands of Galvatron. He is able to think about plans and strategies, and capable of speech. Like the Angels, Angel-Scream is able to generate an A.T.Field and make energy attacks.

History

Transformers Mode “Eva”

Aboard Sky Lynx, Optimus Prime and the Autobots arrive in Tokyo-3 to help defeat an Angel and prevent a new Second Impact. But the ghost of Starscream possessing an Angel appears in front of them: Angel-Scream.

At the NERV, Misato Katsuragi orders the deployment of the Evangelion Unit-01 and the withdraw of the city. Optimus Prime is impressed by human technology and even more so when he faces the Eva. But feeling that he was not in front of a robot, Optimus scans the EVA-01 and transforms to adapt its appearance and size: Optimus Prime: EVA Mode. Now able to fight against Starscream, Optimus faces the enemy.

Angel-Scream attacks Unit-01 with an energy shot, and Optimus Prime moves to protect it before retaliating with his blaster, but the enemy defends itself with an A.T.Field. The Angel-Transformer then lifts Optimus by the throat and proclaims that after killing the leader of the Autobots, he will conquer the Decepticons, and then the universe. He attacks Optimus' helmet until it cracks. The other Transformers try to help their leader, but they are powerless in the face of the disproportionate size of the Angel. It is then that Wheeljack picks up a radio signal from Misato. She tells them that Unit-01 no longer reacts and entrusts the end of the fight to the Transformers before providing them with a strategy.

Bumblebee provokes Angel-Scream by turning in circles around his feet. The Angel tries to crush him, but loses his balance and falls. Misato takes advantage of this moment and the Tokyo-3 buildings are transformed to keep the enemy on the ground. Optimus manages to free himself from Angel-Scream's grip and gives a punch that fails to break his A.T. Field. He then switches to his Energon axe, which breaks through and hits his opponent. Angel-Scream's Core shatters, exploding into a sheaf of blood and causes his ghost to be expelled out. https://tfwiki.net/wiki/Starscream_(G1)#Transformers_.C3.97_Evangelion 

Trivia

Starscream's ghost has a long history of possessing others in the Transformers series.
The Angel that Starcream possessed is the Fourth Angel of Rebuild of Evangelion judging by the first illustration.
